rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BOLANO VS. BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY, BUSINESS FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings;Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 22, 2010, line 3. DEFENDANTS BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, SALVADOR MARROQUIN'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. The Court's prior ruling on the motion for summary adjudication determined as a matter of law only that no triable issue of material fact existed as to whether plaintiff's termination was based on age descrimination. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Aurora, S.A. Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 22, 2010, line 8. Petitioner Aurora, S.A.'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate is denied. ASA has not shown that the insurance commissioner abused his discretion in denying REALIC'S Form A application to purchase Aurora. The court exercises it independent judgment in deciding this question of law and interpreting the statute. The commissioner's denial on the basis of Insurance Code section 1215.2(d)(5) is upheld. The approval of the sale would endanger, diminish, or destroy the Aurora policy holders' judgment against Artemis/ASA in the event the litigation is settled. It is not an abuse of discretion for the commissioner to conclude that REALIC's placing of its corporate interests over the interests of its policyholders harm the polcyholders and the public and show a lack of integrity. The commissioner's denial based on CIC section 1215.2(d)(4) is upheld. REALIC's dividend plans comprise a major change in operations that would prejudice Aurora policy-holders and would enable Artemis and ASA to evade the current dividend restrictions. The restrictions are necessary to maintain capital to satisfy any judgment entered against Artemis, and releasing the dividend restriction is neither fair nor reasonable to policy holders. The commissioner did not abuse his discretion under CIC section 1215.2(d)(1) in his finding of material deficiency with respect to its competence, character and integrity pursuant to CIC section 717(e) as discussed above. However, insufficient evidence supports the commissioner's finding under CIC section 717(f). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HEAREE "HEDDIE" CHU VS. JAMES D. POPE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 22, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANTS JAMES POPE, POPE & POPE PROPERTIES LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INGRIS KARINA LIPTAY VS. COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 22, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action. Plaintiff must clarify which defendants are implicated in each cause of action and state facts showing how C.H.W. is a state actor. It also appears that the letter from C.H.W. sent concurrently with the reply and addressed to the clerk of the court may only have been sent to Dept. 302. Any letter and request for a hearing should be addressed to the clerk's office in Room 103. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. RAYMOND XIANG ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Cathay Bank'S Order To Show Cause Why The Appointment Of A Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed (Per Ex Parte Order Of 06/28/10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 22, 2010, line 7. Plaintiff Cathay Bank's Order To Show Cause Why The Appointment Of A Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed (Per Ex Parte Order Of 06/28/10). Granted. Plaintiff has made a sufficient showing demonstrating a receiver is necessary to secure and preserve subject property 2, which defendants abandoned in an incomplete and hazardous condition. Receiver also appointed for properties 1 and 3. Defendants agreed to the appointment of a receiver in the underlying loan documents. Defendants have not demonstrated to the Court that less drastic remedies are available or appropriate. =(302/CWW)
